LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT. GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE PLAINTIFFS DID NOT OPPOSE THIS MOTION, THEY TRIED TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH WAS PROPERLY REJECTED. THE COURT HAS CONSTRUED PLAINTIFFS' UNAUTHORIZED FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT AS A REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH IS GRANTED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Set For Trial On Aug-27-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Stcas Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint is GRANTED with ten days leave to amend. While plaintiffs did not oppose this motion, they tried to file an amended complaint, which was properly rejected. The court has construed plaintiffs' unauthorized filing of an amended complaint as a request for leave to file an amended complaint, which is granted. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
